# Hymoex

> Hybrid Modular Coordinated Experts — A cognitive architecture for scalable multi-agent systems.

---

# Installation

Install the Hymoex type definitions and configure your development environment.

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.10 or higher
- uv (recommended) or pip

## Install from the monorepo

```
cd packages/hymoex-python
uv sync
```

## Install with pip

```
# Core type definitions (pydantic v2 + typing-extensions)
pip install -e .

# With all optional framework dependencies (Pydantic AI, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen)
pip install -e ".[all]"

# Development tools (pytest, ruff, mypy, pre-commit)
pip install -e ".[dev]"
```

## Optional LLM providers

Hymoex uses a provider abstraction so you can plug in any LLM backend. Install the SDK for the provider you want to use:

```
# OpenAI
pip install openai

# Anthropic
pip install anthropic
```

Set the corresponding environment variable:

```
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..."
```

## Verify installation

```python
import hymoex
print(hymoex.__version__)  # 0.1.0
```

## Project layout

```
packages/hymoex-python/       # type definitions (pip install hymoex)
  src/hymoex/
    protocols/                 # Role enum, AgentProtocol, SignalVector
    messaging/                 # 7 HIP message types (pydantic v2)
    agents/                    # Manager, Supervisor, Expert, Executor, Perceiver, Integrator, ExpertManager
    moe/                       # Softmax gating, Rational-Raffle fusion
    modalities/                # OneLineMoE, OneLineSupervisor, MultiLine, migration
    analysis/                  # Topology classification and validation
    providers/                 # LLM abstraction (Mock, OpenAI, Anthropic)
packages/research/             # Benchmarks and paper
apps/sandbox/                  # Interactive sandbox (CLI, notebooks)
apps/docs/                     # Documentation site
```

---

# Quickstart

Build and run your first Hymoex multi-agent system in under five minutes.

## Minimal example

```python
import asyncio
from hymoex import Manager, Supervisor, Expert, OneLineSupervisor, MsgPacket

async def main():
    manager = Manager(objective="Customer support", strategy="route_by_domain")
    supervisor = Supervisor(routing="dependency_aware")

    legal = Expert(domain="legal", skills=["contract_review"])
    tech = Expert(domain="tech", skills=["diagnostics"])
    billing = Expert(domain="billing", skills=["invoicing"])

    system = OneLineSupervisor(
        manager=manager,
        supervisor=supervisor,
        experts=[legal, tech, billing],
    )

    message = MsgPacket(channel="web", customer_id="c1", payload="Invoice mismatch")
    config = system.to_config()  # Export topology as JSON for your framework
    print(result.reply_draft)

asyncio.run(main())
```

## Auto-modality selection

Use auto_select_modality to determine the right modality based on expert count:

```python
from hymoex import ExpertSpec, auto_select_modality

experts = [
    ExpertSpec(domain="legal", skills=["contracts"]),
    ExpertSpec(domain="tech", skills=["diagnostics"]),
    ExpertSpec(domain="billing", skills=["invoicing"]),
    ExpertSpec(domain="hr", skills=["onboarding"]),
]

modality = auto_select_modality(experts)  # Returns "m1", "m2", or "m3"
```

auto_select_modality rules:
- k <= 2: M1 (One-Line MoE)
- 3-5: M2 (One-Line Supervisor)
- > 5: M3 (MoE MultiLine)

## LLM integration

Hymoex does not manage LLM calls — your chosen framework handles execution. Each framework has its own LLM configuration:

- Pydantic AI: Agent("openai:gpt-4o-mini", ...)
- LangGraph: ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")
- CrewAI: Agent(llm="gpt-4o-mini", ...)
- AutoGen: AssistantAgent(llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o-mini"})

See packages/examples/frameworks/ for complete examples per framework.

## Topology output

```python
config = system.to_config()  # Export topology as JSON for your framework

result.reply_draft   # The expert's response text
result.confidence    # 0.0-1.0 confidence score
result.expert_id     # Which expert produced the response
result.tokens_used   # Token count for cost tracking
```

---

# Architecture Overview

Hymoex (Hybrid Modular Coordinated Experts) is a cognitive architecture for building scalable multi-agent systems. It combines Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) gating with a hierarchical agent taxonomy and progressive deployment modalities.

## Core principles

- Clean Architecture: each layer has clearly defined responsibilities
- SOLID principles: every agent fulfills a single role; the system is extensible without restructuring
- Asynchronous by default: all agent processing is async, enabling parallel expert execution
- Framework-agnostic: provider abstraction supports OpenAI, Anthropic, and patterns for Pydantic AI, LangGraph, CrewAI, and AutoGen

## The 7-role agent taxonomy

| Role           | Class          | Layer        | Responsibility                                              |
|----------------|----------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
| Manager        | Manager        | Strategic    | Sets global objectives, determines routing strategy         |
| Integrator     | Integrator     | Coordination | Routes context across domains, manages SLA budgets (M3)     |
| Expert Manager | ExpertManager  | Coordination | Orchestrates MoE gating across expert pools (M3)            |
| Supervisor     | Supervisor     | Tactical     | Coordinates expert delegation, manages task dependencies    |
| Expert         | Expert         | Knowledge    | Provides domain-specific knowledge                          |
| Executor       | Executor       | Operational  | Performs external actions (API calls, payments)              |
| Perceiver      | Perceiver      | Operational  | Captures environmental telemetry and feedback loops         |

## Agent base class

All agents extend BaseAgent, a Pydantic v2 model:

```python
class BaseAgent(BaseModel):
    id: str           # Auto-generated 8-char hex
    role: Role        # One of the 7 roles
    config: dict      # Arbitrary configuration
    llm: LLMProvider | None  # Optional LLM backend

    async def process(self, message: Any) -> Any: ...
```

## Communication: the HIP protocol

Agents communicate via the Hymoex Interchange Protocol (HIP), which defines 7 typed message models (all Pydantic v2):

- MsgPacket: Raw user input with channel metadata
- ContextEnvelope: Enriched packet with routing state and SLA budget
- DecisionRequest: Signal vector plus context for expert selection
- ExpertPayload: Expert response with confidence and audit trail
- ExecCommand: Typed action command (CRM update, payment, etc.)
- ExecReceipt: Execution result with latency and status
- Telemetry: Real-time feedback (sentiment, delivery status)

## Three deployment modalities

M1: Manager -> Expert(s) -> Executors/Perceivers          (k <= 2 experts)
M2: Manager -> Supervisor -> Expert(s)                     (3-5 experts)
M3: Manager -> Integrators -> Expert Manager -> Teams      (5+ experts)

## MoE gating

The Mixture-of-Experts gate uses a softmax function: g(s) = softmax(Ws + b)

Where s is a composite signal vector composed of intent embeddings, emotion vectors, stage flags, and lead scores. The gate selects the top-k experts and routes the request to them in parallel.

## Progressive migration

Systems can scale from M1 to M3 without rewriting agents:

```python
from hymoex import migrate_m1_to_m2, migrate_m2_to_m3

m2 = migrate_m1_to_m2(m1, additional_experts=[expert_c])
m3 = migrate_m2_to_m3(m2)
```

---

# Deployment Modalities

Hymoex supports three deployment modalities. The choice depends on the number of domain experts and the complexity of your workflow.

## M1: One-Line MoE

Use when you have 1-2 domain experts and simple parallel tasks.

Structure: Manager -> Expert(s) -> Executors/Perceivers

```python
from hymoex import Manager, Expert, OneLineMoE, MsgPacket

system = OneLineMoE(
    manager=Manager(objective="Support"),
    experts=[
        Expert(domain="legal", skills=["contracts"]),
        Expert(domain="tech", skills=["diagnostics"]),
    ],
)

validation = validate_topology(
    MsgPacket(channel="web", customer_id="c1", payload="Contract question")
)
```

Class signature:
- manager: Manager
- experts: list[Expert] (min 1, max 2)
- executors: list[Executor] (optional)
- perceivers: list[Perceiver] (optional)
- gating_config: GatingConfig

## M2: One-Line Supervisor

Use when you have 3-5 domain experts and need coordinated workflows.

Structure: Manager -> Supervisor -> Expert(s)

```python
from hymoex import Manager, Supervisor, Expert, OneLineSupervisor, MsgPacket

system = OneLineSupervisor(
    manager=Manager(objective="Support"),
    supervisor=Supervisor(routing="dependency_aware"),
    experts=[
        Expert(domain="legal", skills=["contracts"]),
        Expert(domain="tech", skills=["diagnostics"]),
        Expert(domain="billing", skills=["invoicing"]),
    ],
)

validation = validate_topology(
    MsgPacket(channel="web", customer_id="c1", payload="Invoice question")
)
```

Class signature:
- manager: Manager
- supervisor: Supervisor
- experts: list[Expert] (min 3, max 5)
- executors: list[Executor] (optional)
- perceivers: list[Perceiver] (optional)
- gating_config: GatingConfig

## M3: MoE MultiLine

Use when you have 5+ experts organized into multiple teams.

Structure: Manager -> Integrator(s) -> Expert Manager -> Team(s)

Introduces Integrator (routes context across domains) and Expert Manager (coordinates expert selection across the global pool).

```python
from hymoex import (
    Manager, Integrator, ExpertManager, Supervisor,
    Expert, MultiLine, Team, MsgPacket,
)

system = MultiLine(
    manager=Manager(objective="Enterprise support"),
    integrators=[Integrator()],
    expert_manager=ExpertManager(),
    teams=[
        Team(
            name="sales",
            supervisor=Supervisor(routing="dependency_aware"),
            experts=[
                Expert(domain="pricing", skills=["quotes"]),
                Expert(domain="crm", skills=["lead_scoring"]),
            ],
        ),
        Team(
            name="operations",
            supervisor=Supervisor(routing="dependency_aware"),
            experts=[
                Expert(domain="logistics", skills=["routing"]),
                Expert(domain="inventory", skills=["stock_check"]),
                Expert(domain="fulfillment", skills=["shipping"]),
            ],
        ),
    ],
)
```

## Comparison

| Property       | M1              | M2                   | M3               |
|----------------|-----------------|----------------------|-------------------|
| Expert count   | k <= 2          | 3-5                  | 5+                |
| Supervisor     | No              | Yes                  | Yes (per team)    |
| Integrator     | No              | No                   | Yes               |
| Expert Manager | No              | No                   | Yes               |
| Use case       | Simple parallel | Coordinated workflows | Enterprise-scale  |

---

# MoE Gating

The MoE (Mixture-of-Experts) gating mechanism selects the most relevant experts for a given request and fuses their responses.

## Pipeline overview

1. Signal extraction: build a composite signal vector from the request
2. Softmax scoring: compute selection probabilities for each expert
3. Top-k selection: pick the k highest-scoring experts

After the selected experts process the request in parallel, their responses are fused using the Rational-Raffle mechanism.

## Signal vector

```python
class SignalVector(BaseModel):
    intent_embedding: list[float]
    emotion_embedding: list[float]
    stage_flag: list[float]
    lead_score: float

    def to_vector(self) -> list[float]:
        return self.intent_embedding + self.emotion_embedding + self.stage_flag + [self.lead_score]
```

## Softmax gate

g(s) = softmax(Ws + b)

Where s is the signal vector, W is the expert weight matrix, b is an optional bias.

```python
from hymoex.moe.gate import softmax_gate, GatingConfig

config = GatingConfig(temperature=1.0, top_k=2)
scores = softmax_gate(signal, expert_weights, config=config)
```

## GatingConfig

```python
class GatingConfig(BaseModel):
    temperature: float = 1.0          # Softmax temperature
    top_k: int = 2                    # Number of experts to select
    low_temperature: float = 0.5      # Temperature under SLA pressure
    sla_threshold_ms: float = 500.0   # Budget threshold for temperature switch
```

## Dynamic temperature

When SLA budget is running low, the gate switches to low_temperature for sharper expert selection.

## Convenience function

```python
from hymoex.moe.gate import gate_experts

result = gate_experts(
    request=decision_request,
    expert_weights={"expert_a": [1.0], "expert_b": [0.5]},
    config=GatingConfig(top_k=2),
)

result.selected_experts  # ["expert_a", "expert_b"]
result.weights           # {"expert_a": 0.72, "expert_b": 0.28}
```

## Rational-Raffle fusion

score(r_i) = alpha_i * relevance(r_i, m) * confidence(r_i)

Where alpha_i is the gating weight, relevance is Jaccard similarity, and confidence is the expert's self-reported confidence.

```python
from hymoex.moe.fusioner import rational_raffle, FusionConfig

result = rational_raffle(
    responses=[(payload_a, 0.72), (payload_b, 0.28)],
    request=decision_request,
    config=FusionConfig(threshold=0.3),
)

result.base_response      # Highest-scoring ExpertPayload
result.augmented_content  # Additional responses above threshold
result.scores             # Score per expert
result.reason_vector_hash # SHA-256 audit trail
```

---

# Agents API

All agents extend BaseAgent and implement the AgentProtocol. Each agent has a fixed Role, a unique ID, and an optional LLM provider.

## BaseAgent

```python
class BaseAgent(BaseModel):
    id: str                      # Auto-generated 8-char hex UUID
    role: Role                   # One of the 7 roles
    config: dict[str, Any]       # Arbitrary configuration
    llm: LLMProvider | None      # Optional LLM backend

    async def process(self, message: Any) -> Any: ...
```

## Role enum

```python
class Role(str, Enum):
    MANAGER = "manager"
    INTEGRATOR = "integrator"
    EXPERT_MANAGER = "expert_manager"
    SUPERVISOR = "supervisor"
    EXPERT = "expert"
    EXECUTOR = "executor"
    PERCEIVER = "perceiver"
```

## Manager

Sets global objectives and determines routing strategy.

- objective: str -- description of the system's goal
- strategy: str -- routing strategy label (default: "default")
- system_prompt: str -- system prompt sent to the LLM when attached
- Input: MsgPacket -> Output: ContextEnvelope

## Supervisor

Coordinates expert delegation and manages task dependencies.

- routing: str -- routing strategy label (default: "dependency_aware")
- Input: ExpertPayload or list[ExpertPayload] -> Output: ExpertPayload (highest confidence)

## Expert

Provides domain-specific knowledge and resolves specialized queries.

- domain: str -- domain label (default: "general")
- skills: list[str] -- list of skill descriptors
- system_prompt: str -- custom system prompt (auto-generated if empty)
- Input: DecisionRequest -> Output: ExpertPayload

Output fields: reply_draft, confidence (0.0-1.0), expert_id, tokens_used

## Executor

Performs external actions (API calls, document generation, payments). Idempotent via idempotency_key.

- Input: ExecCommand -> Output: ExecReceipt

## Perceiver

Captures environmental telemetry and feeds real-time feedback loops.

- Input: ExecReceipt or Any -> Output: Telemetry

## Integrator (M3 only)

Routes context across domain boundaries and manages SLA budgets.

- routing_overhead_ms: float -- ms deducted from SLA budget per hop (default: 50.0)
- Input: ContextEnvelope -> Output: ContextEnvelope

## ExpertManager (M3 only)

Orchestrates MoE gating across expert pools and fuses responses via Rational-Raffle.

- experts: list[Expert] -- the pool of experts to gate over
- gating_config: GatingConfig -- MoE gating configuration
- Input: DecisionRequest -> Output: ExpertPayload

---

# Messaging API

All inter-agent communication uses the HIP (Hymoex Interchange Protocol) -- 7 typed message models (Pydantic v2).

## MsgPacket

Raw user input with channel metadata.

- channel: "whatsapp" | "web" | "voice"
- customer_id: str
- payload: str (the user's message text)
- lang: str = "en"
- latency: float = 0.0
- timestamp: datetime (auto-set)

## ContextEnvelope

Enriched packet with routing state, produced by the Manager.

- msg_packet: MsgPacket
- lead_stage: str = "unknown"
- sla_budget: float = 5000.0
- interaction_id: UUID (auto-generated)
- trace_context: str = ""

## DecisionRequest

Signal vector plus context for MoE gating.

- intent_embedding: list[float]
- emotion_vector: list[float]
- stage_flag: list[float]
- lead_score: float = 0.0
- context: ContextEnvelope | None

## ExpertPayload

Expert response with confidence and audit trail.

- reply_draft: str
- confidence: float (0.0-1.0)
- reason_vector: str (SHA-256 hash from fusion)
- expert_id: str
- tokens_used: int

## ExecCommand

Typed action command sent to Executors.

- type: "crm_update" | "doc_generate" | "payment" | "calendar"
- params: dict[str, Any]
- callback_url: str = ""
- idempotency_key: UUID (auto-generated)

## ExecReceipt

Execution result returned by Executors.

- status: "success" | "failure" | "pending"
- latency: float
- error_code: str | None
- next_action: str | None

## Telemetry

Real-time feedback produced by Perceivers.

- sentiment: float (-1.0 to 1.0)
- delivery_status: str
- action_outcome: str
- team_id: str
- stage: str
- timestamp: datetime (auto-set)

## HIPMessage type alias

Union type covering all 7 message types:
MsgPacket | ContextEnvelope | DecisionRequest | ExpertPayload | ExecCommand | ExecReceipt | Telemetry

---

# Modalities API

## OneLineMoE (M1)

For simple systems with k <= 2 domain experts.

Pipeline: Manager -> Gate -> Experts -> Fuse

```python
class OneLineMoE(BaseModel):
    manager: Manager
    experts: list[Expert]           # min 1, max 2
    executors: list[Executor]       # default: []
    perceivers: list[Perceiver]     # default: []
    gating_config: GatingConfig

    async def run(self, message: MsgPacket) -> ExpertPayload: ...
```

## OneLineSupervisor (M2)

For coordinated systems with 3-5 domain experts.

Pipeline: Manager -> Gate -> Experts -> Fuse -> Supervise

```python
class OneLineSupervisor(BaseModel):
    manager: Manager
    supervisor: Supervisor
    experts: list[Expert]           # min 3, max 5
    executors: list[Executor]       # default: []
    perceivers: list[Perceiver]     # default: []
    gating_config: GatingConfig

    async def run(self, message: MsgPacket) -> ExpertPayload: ...
```

## MultiLine (M3)

For enterprise-scale systems with 5+ experts organized into teams.

Pipeline: Manager -> Integrators -> Gate -> Experts -> Fuse

```python
class Team(BaseModel):
    name: str
    supervisor: Supervisor
    experts: list[Expert]
    executors: list[Executor]
    perceivers: list[Perceiver]

class MultiLine(BaseModel):
    manager: Manager
    integrators: list[Integrator]   # min 1
    expert_manager: ExpertManager
    teams: list[Team]               # min 1
    gating_config: GatingConfig

    def all_experts(self) -> list[Expert]: ...
    async def run(self, message: MsgPacket) -> ExpertPayload: ...
```

## Migration functions

migrate_m1_to_m2(system, supervisor=None, additional_experts=None) -> OneLineSupervisor
  Preserves all existing agents. Adds Supervisor + optional experts.

migrate_m2_to_m3(system, integrators=None, expert_manager=None, team_name="team_1") -> MultiLine
  Preserves all existing agents. Existing Supervisor becomes Team Supervisor.

compute_preservation_ratio(original_agent_count, preserved_agent_count) -> float
  Compute the code preservation ratio (100% agent preservation).

## Auto-selection

auto_select_modality(experts) -> "m1" | "m2" | "m3"
  Based on expert count.

validate_topology(system) -> {"valid": bool, "warnings": list}
  Validates that a topology meets all modality constraints.

---

# Migration Guide

Hymoex supports progressive migration between modalities. Start simple and scale up, preserving 100% of existing agent definitions at each step.

## When to migrate

- M1 -> M2: Expert count exceeds 2
- M2 -> M3: Expert count exceeds 5, or multiple teams needed

## Step 1: Start with M1

```python
from hymoex import Manager, Expert, OneLineMoE, MsgPacket

manager = Manager(objective="Customer support")
m1 = OneLineMoE(
    manager=manager,
    experts=[
        Expert(domain="legal", skills=["contracts"]),
        Expert(domain="tech", skills=["diagnostics"]),
    ],
)
```

## Step 2: Migrate to M2

```python
from hymoex import migrate_m1_to_m2, Expert

m2 = migrate_m1_to_m2(m1, additional_experts=[
    Expert(domain="billing", skills=["invoicing"]),
])
```

What gets preserved: Manager, all Experts, Executors, Perceivers, GatingConfig, expert_weights.
What gets added: Supervisor (auto-created or custom), additional Experts.

## Step 3: Migrate to M3

```python
from hymoex import migrate_m2_to_m3

m3 = migrate_m2_to_m3(m2, team_name="support_team")
```

What gets preserved: Manager, Supervisor (becomes Team Supervisor), all agents.
What gets added: Integrator, ExpertManager, Team wrapper.

## Adding teams after migration

```python
from hymoex import Team, Supervisor, Expert

new_team = Team(
    name="sales_team",
    supervisor=Supervisor(routing="dependency_aware"),
    experts=[Expert(domain="pricing", skills=["quotes"])],
)
m3.teams.append(new_team)
```

## Verifying preservation

```python
from hymoex.modalities.migration import compute_preservation_ratio

ratio = compute_preservation_ratio(original_count, preserved_count)
```

---

# LLM Providers

Hymoex uses a provider abstraction (LLMProvider protocol) so you can plug in any LLM backend. Without a provider, agents return deterministic stub responses.

## Provider protocol

```python
class LLMProvider(Protocol):
    async def complete(self, system_prompt, user_message, temperature=0.7, max_tokens=1024) -> LLMResponse: ...
    async def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]: ...

class LLMResponse(BaseModel):
    content: str
    tokens_used: int
    model: str
    metadata: dict[str, Any]
```

## LLM Integration

Hymoex does not manage LLM calls — your chosen framework handles execution.
Each framework has its own LLM configuration:

```python
# Pydantic AI
agent = Agent("openai:gpt-4o-mini", system_prompt="You are a legal expert.")

# LangGraph
llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# CrewAI
agent = Agent(role="Legal Expert", llm="gpt-4o-mini")

# AutoGen
agent = AssistantAgent("legal", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o-mini"})
```

## Framework examples

```
pip install -e ".[pydantic-ai]"    # Pydantic AI
pip install -e ".[langgraph]"      # LangGraph
pip install -e ".[crewai]"         # CrewAI
pip install -e ".[autogen]"        # AutoGen
pip install -e ".[all]"            # All of the above
```
